prerequisites: A pretix account on the hosted service (pretix.eu) or a self-hosted pretix installation you can administer.
An organizer account with at least one team you can administer, since API tokens are created at the team level and inherit that team's permissions.
The token value generated for the team, which is shown only once at creation time and cannot be retrieved again afterward.

authMethods.name: API token (Team token, api_token)
authMethods.summary: pretix uses team-level API tokens for server-to-server access. The token is created within an organizer team and inherits that team's permissions. Supply it in the connector's api_token field. pretix expects it on each request in an HTTP header of the form 'Authorization: Token <your-token>'.

setupSteps.title: Open the team that should own the token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to pretix and go to your organizer's team settings. Create a new team or select an existing one. The token inherits whatever permissions you grant that team, so choose or configure a team scoped to the access the connector needs.

setupSteps.title: Generate the API token
setupSteps.body: In the team detail view, use the form below the list of team members to add a new API token. Enter a description to identify it, then click Add. pretix shows the token in a success message. Copy it immediately, because pretix will not let you retrieve it again.

setupSteps.title: Provide the token to the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the generated token into the connector's required api_token field. This is the only credential pretix needs; the connector sends it as the 'Authorization: Token <api_token>' header on every request to the pretix REST API (base URL https://pretix.eu/api/v1/ for the hosted service, with organizer and event slugs in the request paths).
